details.text: there is a pattern when the surah beginning with the huroof al-muqatta’aat (disjointed letters) begins with a طاء like in this surah طسٓمٓ in that musa (as) will be discussed in it.
Derivative: فُؤَادُ refers to the emotional heart that can get overwhelmed.
Derivative: قُصِّيهِ from قَصَّ means: To follow the footsteps or traces i.e., to follow closely.
- عَن جُنُبٍ means to be At a little bit of a distance away.
Derivative: اِسْتَوَى – Means to straighten and become upright. Here it can mean to mature and ripen in terms of knowledge and body i.e., that he became strong in knowledge and physically.
Derivative: وَكَزَ means to punch someone anywhere from the neck down.
Derivative: اسْتَغَاثَ: غَيْث is timely rain i.e., when it is needed. اسْتَغَاثَ is to ask for help at the critical moment or time of need.
Derivative: غَوِىٌّ is someone who incites and entices trouble taking advantage of someone’s confusion, goodwill or innocence.

Derivative: يَتَرَقَّبُ. رَقيب is someone who is vigilant and watchful wary of a danger that needs to be protected or guarded from. يَتَرَقَّبُ means to keep watch, be on guard and look out for yourself from a danger that could come from anywhere.
Derivative: اِسْتَنصَرَ mans to ask for aid and also to pretend to ask for aid.
Derivative: يَبْطِشَ to take hold of and seize forcefully or to act violently.
Derivative: يَأْتَمِرُونَ from اِئتمرَ means to confer, deliberate or hold a meeting to discuss punitive measures.
- سَوَاءَ السَّبِيلِ means the direct route and straightest of paths. That path which leads to the destination in the easiest manner possible.
Derivative: تَذُودَانِ from ذَادَ – يَذُودُ and it means to hold back to the best of your ability i.e., implies a struggle. Here it means that the two women were struggling to keep their flock back.
Derivative: فَقِيرٌ to be destitute and have nothing. فَقْر means for the backbone to be broken by burden, difficulty or calamity. Here فَقِيرٌ means to be in a great or dire need.
Derivative: اسْتِحْيَاءٍ means a state of shyness and it implies modesty and the guarding of the حَياء (shame and modesty).
Derivative: أَشُقَّ from شَقَّ which means to place a burden upon you or put you in difficulty or hardship.
Derivative: وَكِيلٌ – It means the one who watches over and takes cares of something. The disposer and executor of something. Reliance is implied towards the one who is the وَكِيلٌ (Wakeel).
Derivative: جَذْوَةٍ something with some fire in it like a piece of coal or wood with the redness of fire in it, which can then be used to make a fire elsewhere.

Derivative: سَارَ means to set off, journey, travel and implies ease and casualness. The use of it here in this context indicates to the ease of Musa’s departure from the household of his father-in-law in Madyan after he had fulfilled his term.
- بِٱلْحَقِّ means “With the truth” or “In truth” but also carries the meaning of “With purpose” i.e., there is a reason and point behind telling you this story.
Derivative: شَاطِئِ is the side or edge of something and is used commonly for the shore or coast of the sea i.e., the edge of the sea.
Derivative: أَقْبِلْ means to come forward and here it carries the meaning of coming forward and to face what is before you and possibly your fears.
Derivative: الرَّهْب refers to fear and its continuation.
Derivative: رِدْءًا refers to a prop or support structure that supports or props up something else to keep it upright, firm and so that it doesn't fall down.
Derivative: عَضُد refers to your arm from you elbow to the shoulder.
Derivative: نَشُدُّ from شَدَّ. It means to tie up and tighten i.e., to make firm and strong.
- شَدَّ العَضَد which literally means to tighten the upper arm means to strengthen, fortify or reinforce.

- It can be observed that the feminine form of the verb تَكُونُ is used here with عَاقِبَةُ (Outcome or Final) whilst the masculine has been used in other places attached to عَاقِبَةُ like in the example: فَانظُرُوا كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الْمُجْرِمِينَ “See how the end or outcome (عَاقِبَةُ) of the criminals was (كَانَ masculine form)?”. It is understood that whenever عَاقِبَةُ is connected to the masculine form, it is related to punishment العَذاب (which is masculine) and in all other cases عَاقِبَةُ refers to the good outcome in the hereafter i.e., reward and paradise.

Derivative: إِلَٰهٍ does not only refer to a deity, entity or being that is worshipped in the form of prayers and rituals but also includes that which is followed and obeyed i.e., the One to whom absolute obedience belongs.

Derivative: شِيَعًا means “groups” and “factions”. A people (Qawm) that has come together over an affair, matter or set of ideas or objectives. شِيَعًا is the plural of شِيعَة which is a group that views itself to be distinct from others and shares a common thinking.
Derivative: عَلَا means to be high i.e., of a high position and power in the land. It also means to be arrogant i.e., he acted arrogantly in the land with his power.
Derivative: نَبَذْنا from نَيَذَ which means to hurl or throw something worthless away i.e., to toss away or discard.
Derivative: ٱلْمَقْبُوحِينَ: مَقْبُوح is someone who has been made ugly and deformed. It means from those who are contemptible and disgraced due to their ugly acts and distanced from all goodness.

Derivative: بَصَآئِرَ means things to see i.e., that which the people may benefit from. It is the plural of بَصِيرَة which means an insight i.e., the Kitab was revealed to Musa as a means of insight for the people.
Derivative: ثَاوِيًا is from ثَوَى which means to reside and implies residing or living in the same place as your father who lived in the same place as his father and so on i.e., for generations.

Derivative: وَصَّلْنَا from وَصَّلَ – تَوْصِيل which means to make one thing or matter connect to another seamlessly i.e., that the word of the Tawrah flows into the word of the Qur’an and as such is (obviously) from the same source.
Derivative: نُتَخَطَّفْ from تَخَطَّفَ which means to be snatched away, like a bird that snatches its prey, or to be swept away. In this verse it means to become targets and be picked off.
Derivative: يُجْبَىٰ means to be brought and imply that the thing being brought is a select or choice variety.
Derivative: مَعِيشَة refers to a good life.
Derivative: بَطِرَتْ from بَطِرَ means to be wanton and reckless i.e., to squander the good that had been provided.
- the مِن here in مِن قَرْيَةٍ implies a diversity of towns i.e., “How many different kinds of towns have we destroyed”.

Derivative: ظَالِمُونَ – The use of ظَالِمُونَ the اِسْم (noun) instead of the verb ظَلَمُوا indicates that this was their continuous state and condition, that they were persistent upon that and were non-changing.
Derivative: أُمٌّ means mother and in respect to towns and cities, it means its major or capital cities.
Derivative: نُمَكِّنَ from مَكَّنَ means to consolidate, establish and give authority i.e., in the land by being in charge.
Derivative: الدُّنْيَا refers to the worldly life but also carries the meaning of being lower and inferior from دَنَا i.e., from the two lives this is the inferior one.
Derivative: مَتَاعُ in origin is something that you gain utility from – a utensil, good, baggage etc. It comprises of two elements: One is usage and the second is enjoyment.
Derivative: تَزْعُمُونَ means to have false confidence and to have pretence and to feign, claim or allege something which is baseless. It always has a negative connotation when used in the Qur’an.
Derivative: تَبَرَّأْنَا from تَبَرَّأَ which means to dissociate from, disown or declare yourselves free from something or others.

Derivative: أَوْحَيْنَآ from أَوْحَى which means to give وَحْي (divine revelation or inspiration). To distinguish between the وَحْي (Wahy) that is given to Prophets and Messengers and the وَحْي (Wahy) given to others like that given to the mother of Musa (as) the word إِلْهام (inspiration) has been used by scholars to refer to the latter.
Derivative: سَرْمَدًا refers something that is perpetual, has no beginning or end and never changes.

Derivative: تَفْرَحْ from فَرِحَ which means to be happy, joyful and rejoiceful. With the negative لَا تَفْرَحْ as used in this verse “Don’t rejoice” it carries the meaning of don’t be overly happy or don’t show off, gloat and be full of yourself or your situation and abundant riches.
Derivative: الْعُصْبَةِ A strong band of people with physical prowess.

Derivative: تَنُوءُ from نَاءَ means to have to lift or carry something that is a heavy load or burden which requires expending great effort to the point of exhaustion i.e., that can barely be lifted due to its weight.
Derivative: أَحْسِن can mean to excel in your conduct and do your best or it can mean to be good to others.
Derivative: ٱلْتَقَطَ means to pick something up from the ground.
Derivative: خَسَفْنَا from خَسَفَ which means to sink down into the ground or soil and disappear.
Derivative: يَقْدِرُ from قَدَرَ means: To restrict, constrict or limit.
Derivative: رَادُّ from رَدَّ which means to bring back or return someone or something to where it came from.
